commit.message:
Add automatic `flyway` label for PRs touching Flyway migrations (#1280)

### **Behov / Bakgrunn**

PRs som endrer Flyway-migreringer under
`migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/` ble ikke automatisk
merket, noe som gjorde det vanskelig å raskt identifisere
databaseendringer i PR-oversikten.

### **Løsning**

- Legger til `.github/labeler.yml` med glob-regel som trigges på
endringer i `migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**`
- Legger til `.github/workflows/labeler.yml` som kjører
`actions/labeler@v6` på alle PRs og setter `flyway`-labelen automatisk

Forutsetter at `flyway`-labelen er opprettet manuelt i repoet.

### **Andre endringer**

Ingen.

### **Skjermbilder** (hvis relevant)

N/A

> `migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/`
> 
> ## Files to create
> 
> ### `.github/labeler.yml`
> ```yaml
> flyway:
>   - changed-files:
> - any-glob-to-any-file:
'migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**'
> ```
>

feat: ICL (In-Context Learning) mode for voice cloning (#32) (#33)

* feat: add ref_text ICL mode support for voice cloning (#32)

Implement In-Context Learning (ICL) mode for higher-quality voice cloning
when both reference text and reference audio are provided.

Changes:
- New SpeechTokenizer.cs: ONNX wrapper for tokenizer12hz_encode.onnx,
  encodes raw 24kHz audio into codec codes [1, T, 16]
- LanguageModel.cs: New GenerateWithSpeakerEmbeddingAndRefText() method,
  BuildPrefillEmbedding inserts ICL embeddings (ref text + ref audio codes)
  between codec prefix and target text in prefill sequence
- VoiceClonePipeline.cs: New SynthesizeAsync overload with refText param,
  lazy-loaded SpeechTokenizer, backward-compatible API
- VoiceCloningDownloader.cs: Added tokenizer12hz_encode.onnx to downloads
- MelSpectrogram.cs: Made ReadWav/Resample internal for code reuse

All 245 existing tests pass. Fully backward compatible.

commit.message:
Feature parity: vim editor + shell with pipes/redirection

led.osc: Full vim-style modal editor with NORMAL/INSERT/COMMAND modes.
  - hjkl movement, w/b word motion, 0/$ line ends, gg/G file ends
  - i/a/I/A/o/O insert entry, x delete, dd/yy/p/P yank-paste
  - J join, ~ case toggle, numeric prefixes (e.g. 5dd)
  - :w/:q/:q!/:wq/:x/:e commands, Ctrl-F/Ctrl-B page movement
  - Non-blocking input loop, dirty-flag rendering, cursor shape
  - CRLF detection and preservation

sh.osc: Shell with I/O redirection and pipes via 6 new builtins.
  - > >> < redirection using fd_dup/fd_dup2
  - cmd1 | cmd2 pipes using pipe_create/proc_spawn/proc_wait
  - Quote-aware argument parsing (single quotes)
  - PATH search via path_find_exec

New builtins: fd_dup, fd_dup2, proc_spawn, proc_wait, pipe_create,
path_find_exec — added across semantic.rs, codegen.rs, osc_runtime.h,
and osc_runtime.c with freestanding/POSIX/Windows implementations.

commit.message:
Fix ev formulas (#52)

* fix: improve EV formulas with one-level lookahead

Check EV now models villain continuation (check-back vs bet) with hero
optimally calling or folding when facing a bet, instead of the simplistic
equity * pot formula that assumed immediate showdown.

Bet/raise EV now allows hero to call villain's raise when profitable,
instead of always assuming hero folds (which made raise_branch = -bet).

Extracted compute_check_ev() and compute_aggressive_ev() as testable
helper functions with 13 unit tests covering edge cases and formula
correctness.

Closes #49

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: compute postflop raise sizes as multiplier of villain's bet

R3x/R4x/R5x were calculated as 300-500% of pot, resulting in
massive overbets (12-20x villain's bet) that gave hero excessive
fold equity and made calling never optimal.

Now computed as 3x/4x/5x the villain's bet amount, producing
realistic raise sizes (e.g. R3x facing a 33% cbet = 74% pot
instead of 300% pot).

Also fixed pot calculation in the router to handle raises
consistently when they appear in action history.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add pot/sizing debug stats to solver output

Shows pot size (BB), call amount (BB), bet size (BB + %pot) for
each action in the debug panel. Helps diagnose solver issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct pot double-counting in raise EV formula

When hero raises over villain's bet, pot_bb already includes the
villain's prior bet (call_amount_bb). The formula was using
pot_bb + 2*bet_bb which double-counts call_amount_bb.

Fixed by subtracting call_amount_bb from both the call branch
and re-raise branch pot totals. Added call_amount_bb parameter
to compute_aggressive_ev() (defaults to 0 for open bets).

Added test verifying correct pot accounting for raise-over-bet
scenario (R5x facing B33: 23.65 BB not 25.465 BB).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add SPR-aware future value to EV calculations

On early streets, the solver now estimates additional EV from remaining
streets when villain commits chips (calls a bet or bets after a check).
This makes smaller bets more attractive for value hands because they
keep villain in for future streets.

Key mechanics:
- Future value = (2*equity - 1) * remaining_stack * extraction_rate * spr_factor
- Higher SPR (smaller bets) -> more room for future betting -> higher bonus
- Only hands with equity > 50% get the bonus (floored at 0)
- Applied to: bet/call branch, raise/hero-calls branch, check/facing-bet branch
- NOT applied to: fold branches, check-back branch (no committed money)

Example impact (AK on K72r, 100 BB deep):
- Before: R5x (9.57) > B33 (7.48) -- solver always raises big
- After:  B33 (11.22) > R5x (10.72) -- solver prefers smaller bet

Also adds effective stack and SPR to debug panel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* tune: reduce extraction rate and steepen SPR curve

Lower EXTRACTION_RATE from 0.15 to 0.10 to prevent over-slowplaying.
Switch SPR factor from linear (spr/10) to sqrt curve (sqrt(spr/8))
which more steeply penalises low-SPR situations where hero is
pot-committed and has minimal optionality on future streets.

Effect: B33 ≈ R5x ≈ B66 for value hands (~13 BB each), producing
a realistic mixed strategy instead of always slowplaying.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use hard splits for sub-range equity calculations

eval7 ignores combo weights, so the dampening factor (0.3) had no
effect on equity calculations — eq_vs_callers was identical to
eq_vs_full_range. This caused the solver to overbet with marginal
hands because it thought QQ's 63% equity held against callers too.

Added hard_split parameter to filter_range_by_equity(). When True,
combos only appear in their assigned bucket (for equity calc).
When False (default), soft dampening is preserved for cross-street
range narrowing so hands don't drop out too quickly.

EV calculation call sites now use hard_split=True. Range narrowing
in compute_current_ranges() keeps soft splits (hard_split=False).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace AmidiTransport with persistent DryWetMidi connection

- Implement DryWetMidiConnection with persistent input listener,
  address-keyed TCS reply correlator, and PushNotificationReceived event
- Switch DefaultMidiTransport to always use DryWetMidiTransport on all platforms
- Add PushNotificationReceived to IMidiConnection and IKatanaSession
- KatanaSession forwards push events and writes EDITOR_COMMUNICATION_MODE=1
  on connect (tells amp to send unsolicited DT1 push notifications) and 0 on disconnect
- MainWindowViewModel subscribes to push notifications, builds an address-to-action
  lookup at connect time, and applies push values via ApplyDeviceState to update
  the UI without echo-writing back to the amp
- Document BTS architecture, reply correlator, and state update pattern in TECHNICAL_NOTES.md

Each RQ1→DT1 round-trip now takes the actual device latency (~100-200ms)
instead of the amidi recording duration (2s). Full initial load drops from ~30s to ~2-3s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Guard: no config → always redirect to federation-setup

Three layers of protection:
1. SessionStart hook: checks federate.config.json first. If missing, ALL
   federation requests route to setup wizard.
2. federation-orchestration skill: prerequisites section redirects to setup.
3. @federation agent: first instruction is check config, redirect if missing.

No matter which entry point triggers — hook, skill, or agent — a missing
config always lands on the setup wizard.
